text:
In a small sleepy town called Willowbrook lived a quirky old owl named Oliver. He wasn't just any owl. Oliver could chirp in seventeen languages. Instead of feathers, he wore a golden cloak. Every Saturday noon, Oliver would gather all the townsfolk at Whispering Willow.

Now, Willowbrook had more mysteries than you could count. There was the disappearance each autumn. But that's an old story. Today's mystery involved Mr. Jenkins.

Mr. Jenkins lived alone in an old house at the edge of town. One evening, he opened his front door just to get some fresh air. Before he could blink twice, a shadow swept past the door. You see, Mr. Jenkins rarely came out. In fact, nobody had seen him since summer.

Oliver knew they couldn't lose someone that innocent. That night, Oliver perched atop Whispering Willow. Through binoculars, Oliver watched Mr. Jenkins' front door.

Suddenly, a gust filled the night. Leaves spun
